prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: Ollie the octopus was a very shy cephalopod. He lived in a cozy coral cave, deep beneath the swishing, swirling ocean. While other young octopuses zipped and zoomed, playing tag among the anemones or chasing after glimmering schools of fish, Ollie usually just stayed put. He had eight excellent arms, perfect for reaching faraway snacks or giving himself a comforting eight-armed hug, but he rarely used them for waving hello. He desperately wanted to join the games and giggles he heard echoing from the sunlit patches of water, but every time he thought about wiggling out, he felt a little blush of bright pink spread across his soft, squishy skin.
